revision.text: #redirect[[en:Wakamatsu_Ebisu_Shrine]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

{{神社| 名称=若松恵比須神社| 所在地=福岡県北九州市若松区浜町1丁目2-37| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|54|19|N|130|48|46|E|}}| 祭神=事代主命
大国主命| 社格=村社・別表神社| 例祭=[[12月2日]] - [[12月4日|4日]]| 神事=えびす祭}} '''若松恵比須神社'''（わかまつえびすじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[北九州市]][[若松区]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。通称「若松のおえべっさん」。 ==祭神== [[事代主|事代主命]]と[[大国主|大国主命]]を主[[祭神]]とし、[[大山咋神]]・[[天照大神|天照皇大御神]]・[[スサノオ|素盞嗚命]]・[[武内宿禰|武内宿禰命]]を配祀する。 ==歴史== 社伝（『恵比須神社縁起』）によれば、[[仲哀天皇]]と[[神功皇后]]が[[熊襲]]征伐のため洞海から岡津へ船で移動していたとき、急に船が動かなくなったので武内宿禰が海底を調べさせたところ、光り輝く石があったという。この石は熊襲征討にあたっての瑞祥であるとして、これを事代主命の神霊・[[えびす|恵比須神]]として祀ったのが当社の始まりであるとする。このとき、神社を創建した海浜に松を植え、武内宿禰が「松の一面に緑をなして、青々たる海原の溟たる、我心若し」と詠み、これが「若松」の地名の起こりであるとしている。 他に、[[村上天皇]]の時代に蛭子大神宮が創建されたという伝承もある。 [[Category:福岡県の神社|わかまつえひすしんしや]] [[Category:村社|わかまつえひすしんしや]] [[Category:別表神社|わかまつえひすしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=若松恵比須神社| 所在地=福岡県北九州市若松区浜町1丁目2-37| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|54|19|N|130|48|46|E|}}| 祭神=事代主命
大国主命| 社格=村社・別表神社| 例祭=[[12月2日]] - [[12月4日|4日]]| 神事=えびす祭}} '''若松恵比須神社'''（わかまつえびすじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[北九州市]][[若松区]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。通称「若松のおえべっさん」。 ==祭神== [[事代主|事代主命]]と[[大国主|大国主命]]を主[[祭神]]とし、[[大山咋神]]・[[天照大神|天照皇大御神]]・[[スサノオ|素盞嗚命]]・[[武内宿禰|武内宿禰命]]を配祀する。 ==歴史== 社伝（『恵比須神社縁起』）によれば、[[仲哀天皇]]と[[神功皇后]]が[[熊襲]]征伐のため洞海から岡津へ船で移動していたとき、急に船が動かなくなったので武内宿禰が海底を調べさせたところ、光り輝く石があったという。この石は熊襲征討にあたっての瑞祥であるとして、これを事代主命の神霊・[[えびす|恵比須神]]として祀ったのが当社の始まりであるとする。このとき、神社を創建した海浜に松を植え、武内宿禰が「松の一面に緑をなして、青々たる海原の溟たる、我心若し」と詠み、これが「若松」の地名の起こりであるとしている。 他に、[[村上天皇]]の時代に蛭子大神宮が創建されたという伝承もある。 [[Category:福岡県の神社|わかまつえひすしんしや]] [[Category:村社|わかまつえひすしんしや]] [[Category:別表神社|わかまつえひすしんしや]] [[Category:えびす神社|わかまつえひすしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=若松恵比須神社| 所在地=福岡県北九州市若松区浜町1丁目2-37| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|54|19|N|130|48|46|E|}}| 祭神=事代主命
大国主命| 社格=村社・別表神社| 例祭=[[12月2日]] - [[12月4日|4日]]| 神事=えびす祭}} '''若松恵比須神社'''（わかまつえびすじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[北九州市]][[若松区]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。通称「若松のおえべっさん」。 [[若戸大橋]]の高架橋のの真下にあるため、あまり目立たないが、若松の中心街には近い。 ==祭神== [[事代主|事代主命]]と[[大国主|大国主命]]を主[[祭神]]とし、[[大山咋神]]・[[天照大神|天照皇大御神]]・[[スサノオ|素盞嗚命]]・[[武内宿禰|武内宿禰命]]を配祀する。 ==歴史== 社伝（『恵比須神社縁起』）によれば、[[仲哀天皇]]と[[神功皇后]]が[[熊襲]]征伐のため洞海から岡津へ船で移動していたとき、急に船が動かなくなったので武内宿禰が海底を調べさせたところ、光り輝く石があったという。この石は熊襲征討にあたっての瑞祥であるとして、これを事代主命の神霊・[[えびす|恵比須神]]として祀ったのが当社の始まりであるとする。このとき、神社を創建した海浜に松を植え、武内宿禰が「松の一面に緑をなして、青々たる海原の溟たる、我心若し」と詠み、これが「若松」の地名の起こりであるとしている。 他に、[[村上天皇]]の時代に蛭子大神宮が創建されたという伝承もある。 ==交通== *[[北九州市交通局]]バス 若松区役所下車 *[[九州旅客鉄道]][[筑豊本線]] [[若松駅]]から徒歩15分 *[[若戸渡船]] 若松渡場から徒歩5分 [[Category:福岡県の神社|わかまつえひすしんしや]] [[Category:村社|わかまつえひすしんしや]] [[Category:別表神社|わかまつえひすしんしや]] [[Category:えびす神社|わかまつえひすしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=若松恵比須神社| 所在地=福岡県北九州市若松区浜町1丁目2-37| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|54|19|N|130|48|46|E|}}| 祭神=事代主命
大国主命| 社格=村社・別表神社| 例祭=[[12月2日]] - [[12月4日|4日]]| 神事=えびす祭}} '''若松恵比須神社'''（わかまつえびすじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[北九州市]][[若松区]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。通称「若松のおえべっさん」。 [[若戸大橋]]の高架橋のの真下にあるため、あまり目立たないが、若松の中心街には近い。 ==祭神== [[事代主|事代主命]]と[[大国主|大国主命]]を主[[祭神]]とし、[[大山咋神]]・[[天照大神|天照皇大御神]]・[[スサノオ|素盞嗚命]]・[[武内宿禰|武内宿禰命]]を配祀する。 ==歴史== 社伝（『恵比須神社縁起』）によれば、[[仲哀天皇]]と[[神功皇后]]が[[熊襲]]征伐のため洞海から岡津へ船で移動されるｋとき、[[岡県主熊鰐]]が、「天皇様は外海をお進み下さい、皇后様は波静かな内海をお進み下さい。」と奏上したことを受け、皇后様のお船が内海の洞海湾を進まれたとき、急に船が動かなくなったので武内宿禰が海底を調べさせたところ、光り輝く玉の石があがったという。この石こそ海神が皇后様の聖旅を守るみしるしの霊石であるとして、畏み勅命を奉じて、祀った祠が当社の始まりであるとする。その後、再びこの地を訪れた武内宿禰が祠脇の聖地に小松を手植えして「海原の滄溟たる松の青々たるわが心も若し」と歓びの歌を詠み、これが「若松」の地名の起こりであるといわれている。 他に、[[村上天皇]]の時代に蛭子大神宮が創建されたという伝承もある。 ==交通== *[[北九州市交通局]]バス 若松区役所下車 *[[九州旅客鉄道]][[筑豊本線]] [[若松駅]]から徒歩15分 *[[若戸渡船]] 若松渡場から徒歩5分 [[Category:福岡県の神社|わかまつえひすしんしや]] [[Category:村社|わかまつえひすしんしや]] [[Category:別表神社|わかまつえひすしんしや]] [[Category:えびす神社|わかまつえひすしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=若松恵比須神社| 所在地=福岡県北九州市若松区浜町1丁目2-37| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|54|19|N|130|48|46|E|}}| 祭神=事代主命
大国主命| 社格=村社・別表神社| 例祭=[[12月2日]] - [[12月4日|4日]]| 神事=えびす祭}} '''若松恵比須神社'''（わかまつえびすじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[北九州市]][[若松区]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。通称「若松のおえべっさん」。 [[若戸大橋]]の高架橋のの真下にあるため、あまり目立たないが、若松の中心街には近い。 ==祭神== [[事代主|事代主命]]と[[大国主|大国主命]]を主[[祭神]]とし、[[大山咋神]]・[[天照大神|天照皇大御神]]・[[スサノオ|素盞嗚命]]・[[武内宿禰|武内宿禰命]]を配祀する。 ==歴史== 社伝（『恵比須神社縁起』）によれば、[[仲哀天皇]]と[[神功皇后]]が[[熊襲]]征伐のため洞海から岡津へ船で移動するとき、[[岡県主熊鰐]]が、「天皇様は外海をお進み下さい、皇后様は波静かな内海をお進み下さい。」と奏上したことを受け、皇后の船が内海の洞海湾を進んだとき、急に船が動かなくなったので武内宿禰が海底を調べさせたところ、光り輝く玉の石があがったという。この石こそ海神が皇后の聖旅を守るみしるしの霊石であるとして、畏み勅命を奉じて、祀った祠が当社の始まりであるとする。その後、再びこの地を訪れた武内宿禰が祠脇の聖地に小松を手植えして「海原の滄溟たる松の青々たるわが心も若し」と歓びの歌を詠み、これが「若松」の地名の起こりであるといわれている。 他に、[[村上天皇]]の時代に蛭子大神宮が創建されたという伝承もある。 ==交通== *[[北九州市交通局]]バス 若松区役所下車 *[[九州旅客鉄道]][[筑豊本線]] [[若松駅]]から徒歩15分 *[[若戸渡船]] 若松渡場から徒歩5分 {{DEFAULTSORT:わかまつえひすしんしや}} [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:村社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:えびす神社]] [[Category:若松区]] {{shinto-stub}}

revision.text:
{{draft}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg }} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.<ref>{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}</ref> It worships [[Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]]
== History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.<ref name="geocaching-2023a">{{Cite web |last=Geocaching |title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site |url=http://www.geocaching.com/ |access-date=2023-10-19 |website=www.geocaching.com |language=en-US}}</ref>
Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu."<ref name="geocaching-2023a" />
== References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Ebisu]] [[Category:Shtines in kitak]] [[Category:Wakamatsu]] [[Category:Draft articles]] [[Category:[[Category:Original pages]]]] }} [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E6%9D%BE%E6%81%B5%E6%AF%94%E9%A0%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]]

{{draft}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg }} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It worships [[Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==Overview== {{Infobox Shinto shrine | Name = Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine | Image = [[File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg|300px]] {{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=14}} | Location = 1-2-37 Hamamachi, Wakamatsu-ku, [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] | Coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | Festival Deity = [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]
[[Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine Rank = Village shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine]] | Annual Festival = [[December 2]] - [[December 4|4]] | Ceremonies = Ebisu Festival }} '''Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine''' (若松恵比須神社, Wakamatsu Ebisu Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Wakamatsu-ku]], [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine and is currently listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Wakamatsu no Oebessan." The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the [[Wakato Bridge]], making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == Festival Deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[JR Kyushu]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Shinto Shrines in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} [[Category:Beppyo Shrines]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu-ku]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Wakamatsu-ku]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web |last=Geocaching |title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site |url=http://www.geocaching.com/ |access-date=2023-10-19 |website=www.geocaching.com |language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Ebisu]] [[Category:Shtines in kitak]] [[Category:Wakamatsu]] [[Category:[[Category:Original pages]]]] }} [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E6%9D%BE%E6%81%B5%E6%AF%94%E9%A0%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]]

拝殿 |所在地 = [[福岡県]][[北九州市]][[若松区]]浜町1丁目2-37 |位置 = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[事代主命]]
[[大国主命]] |社格=旧[[村社]]・[[別表神社]] |例祭=[[12月2日]] - [[12月4日|4日]] |神事=えびす祭 |地図={{Location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=若松
恵比須神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''若松恵比須神社'''（わかまつえびすじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[北九州市]][[若松区]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[近代社格制度|社格]]は村社で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。通称「若松のおえべっさん」。 [[若戸大橋]]の高架橋の真下にあるため、あまり目立たないが、若松の中心街には近い。 == 祭神 == [[事代主|事代主命]]と[[大国主|大国主命]]を主[[祭神]]とし、[[大山咋神]]・[[天照大神|天照皇大御神]]・[[スサノオ|素盞嗚命]]・[[武内宿禰|武内宿禰命]]を配祀する。 == 歴史 == 社伝（『恵比須神社縁起』）によれば、[[仲哀天皇]]と[[神功皇后]]が[[熊襲]]征伐のため洞海から岡津へ船で移動するとき、[[岡県主熊鰐]]が、「天皇様は外海をお進み下さい、皇后様は波静かな内海をお進み下さい。」と奏上したことを受け、皇后の船が内海の洞海湾を進んだとき、急に船が動かなくなったので武内宿禰が海底を調べさせたところ、光り輝く玉の石があがったという。この石こそ海神が皇后の聖旅を守るみしるしの霊石であるとして、畏み勅命を奉じて、祀った祠が当社の始まりであるとする。その後、再びこの地を訪れた武内宿禰が祠脇の聖地に小松を手植えして「海原の滄溟たる松の青々たるわが心も若し」と歓びの歌を詠み、これが「若松」の地名の起こりであるといわれている。 他に、[[村上天皇]]の時代に蛭子大神宮が創建されたという伝承もある。 == 交通 == *[[北九州市交通局|北九州市営バス]] 若松区役所下車 *[[九州旅客鉄道]][[筑豊本線]] [[若松駅]]から徒歩15分 *[[若戸渡船]] 若松渡場から徒歩5分 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == *[http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ 若松恵比須神社公式Webページ] *[https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ 若松恵比須神社公式Blog] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:わかまつえひすしんしや}} [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:福岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:北九州市の神社]] [[Category:若松区の歴史]] [[Category:若松区の建築物]] [[Category:えびす神社]]

{{draft}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg }} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It worships [[Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==Overview== {{Infobox Shinto shrine | Name = Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine | Image = [[File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg|300px]] {{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=14}} | Location = 1-2-37 Hamamachi, Wakamatsu-ku, [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] | Coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | Festival Deity = [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]
[[Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine Rank = Village shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine]] | Annual Festival = [[December 2]] - [[December 4|4]] | Ceremonies = Ebisu Festival }} '''Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine''' (若松恵比須神社, Wakamatsu Ebisu Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Wakamatsu-ku]], [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine and is currently listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Wakamatsu no Oebessan." The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the [[Wakato Bridge]], making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == Festival Deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[JR Kyushu]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Shinto Shrines in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} [[Category:Beppyo Shrines]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu-ku]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Wakamatsu-ku]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web |last=Geocaching |title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site |url=http://www.geocaching.com/ |access-date=2023-10-19 |website=www.geocaching.com |language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Uncategorized_Shinto_shrines]][[en:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]][[Category:Uncategorized_Shinto_shrines]] [[Category:Ebisu]] [[Category:Shtines in kitak]] [[Category:Wakamatsu]] [[Category:[[Category:Original pages]]]] }} [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E6%9D%BE%E6%81%B5%E6%AF%94%E9%A0%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]]

[[Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web |last=Geocaching |title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site |url=http://www.geocaching.com/ |access-date=2023-10-19 |website=www.geocaching.com |language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[en:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]][[Category:Ebisu]] [[Category:Shtines in kitak]] [[Category:Wakamatsu]] [[Category:[[Category:Original pages]]]] }} [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E6%9D%BE%E6%81%B5%E6%AF%94%E9%A0%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]]

It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It worships [[Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine | image = [[File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg|300px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=14}} | location = 1-2-37 Hamamachi, Wakamatsu-ku, [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | festival deity = [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]
[[Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine]] | annual festival = [[December 2]] - [[December 4|4]] | ceremony = Ebisu festival }} '''Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine''' (若松恵比須神社, Wakamatsu Ebisu Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Wakamatsu-ku]], [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine and is currently listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Wakamatsu no Oebessan." The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the [[Wakato Bridge]], making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == festival deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[JR Kyushu]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Shinto Shrines in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} [[Category:Beppyo Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu-ku]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Wakamatsu-ku]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web |last=Geocaching |title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site |url=http://www.geocaching.com/ |access-date=2023-10-19 |website=www.geocaching.com |language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[en:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]][[Category:Ebisu]] [[Category:Shtines in kitak]] [[Category:Wakamatsu]] [[Category:[[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[en:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]]]] }} [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E6%9D%BE%E6%81%B5%E6%AF%94%E9%A0%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]]

There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[JR Kyushu]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Shinto Shrines in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} [[Category:Beppyo Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu-ku]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Wakamatsu-ku]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. {{draft}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg }} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It worships [[Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine | image = [[File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg|300px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=14}} | location = 1-2-37 Hamamachi, Wakamatsu-ku, [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]
[[Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine]] | festival = [[December 2]] - [[December 4|4]] | ceremony = Ebisu festival }} '''Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine''' (若松恵比須神社, Wakamatsu Ebisu Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Wakamatsu-ku]], [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine and is currently listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Wakamatsu no Oebessan." The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the [[Wakato Bridge]], making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[JR Kyushu]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Shinto Shrines in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} [[Category:Beppyo Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu-ku]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Wakamatsu-ku]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web |last=Geocaching |title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site |url=http://www.geocaching.com/ |access-date=2023-10-19 |website=www.geocaching.com |language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[en:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]][[Category:Ebisu]] [[Category:Shtines in kitak]] [[Category:Wakamatsu]] [[Category:[[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Pages with templates in the wrong namespace]] [[en:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]]]] }} [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E6%9D%BE%E6%81%B5%E6%AF%94%E9%A0%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]]

When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[JR Kyushu]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Shinto Shrines in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} [[Category:Beppyo Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu-ku]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Wakamatsu-ku]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web |last=Geocaching |title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site |url=http://www.geocaching.com/ |access-date=2023-10-19 |website=www.geocaching.com |language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[en:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]][[Category:Ebisu]] [[Category:Shtines in kitak]] [[Category:Wakamatsu]] [[Category:[[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]][[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]][[en:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]]]] }} [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E6%9D%BE%E6%81%B5%E6%AF%94%E9%A0%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]]

The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine and is currently listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Wakamatsu no Oebessan." The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the [[Wakato Bridge]], making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[JR Kyushu]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Shinto Shrines in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu-ku]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Wakamatsu-ku]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web |last=Geocaching |title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site |url=http://www.geocaching.com/ |access-date=2023-10-19 |website=www.geocaching.com |language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:dd]][[en:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]][[Category:Ebisu]] [[Category:Shtines in kitak]] [[Category:Wakamatsu]] [[Category:[[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]][[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]][[Category:dd]][[en:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]]]] }} [[Category:dd]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E6%9D%BE%E6%81%B5%E6%AF%94%E9%A0%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]]

He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:dd]][[en:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]][[Category:Ebisu]] [[Category:Shtines in kitak]] [[Category:Wakamatsu]] [[Category:[[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]][[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]][[Category:dd]][[en:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]]]] }} [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[Category:Unsorted link categories]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E6%9D%BE%E6%81%B5%E6%AF%94%E9%A0%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]]

{{draft}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg }} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It worships [[Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine | image = [[File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg|300px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=14}} | location = 1-2-37 Hamamachi, Wakamatsu-ku, [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]
[[Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine]] | festival = [[December 2]] - [[December 4|4]] | ceremony = Ebisu festival }} '''Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine''' (若松恵比須神社, Wakamatsu Ebisu Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Wakamatsu-ku]], [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine and is currently listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Wakamatsu no Oebessan." The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the [[Wakato Bridge]], making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[JR Kyushu]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Shinto Shrines in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu-ku]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Wakamatsu-ku]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web |last=Geocaching |title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site |url=http://www.geocaching.com/ |access-date=2023-10-19 |website=www.geocaching.com |language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:dd]][[en:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]][[Category:Ebisu]] [[Category:Shtines in kitak]] [[Category:Wakamatsu]] [[Category:[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]][[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]][[Category:dd]][[en:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]]]] }} [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E6%9D%BE%E6%81%B5%E6%AF%94%E9%A0%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]]

When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[JR Kyushu]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Shinto Shrines in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT::Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu-ku]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Wakamatsu-ku]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web |last=Geocaching |title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site |url=http://www.geocaching.com/ |access-date=2023-10-19 |website=www.geocaching.com |language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. {{draft}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg }} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It worships [[Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine | image = [[File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg|300px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=14}} | location = 1-2-37 Hamamachi, Wakamatsu-ku, [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]
[[Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine]] | festival = [[December 2]] - [[December 4|4]] | ceremony = Ebisu festival }} '''Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine''' (若松恵比須神社, Wakamatsu Ebisu Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Wakamatsu-ku]], [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine and is currently listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Wakamatsu no Oebessan." The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the [[Wakato Bridge]], making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[JR Kyushu]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Shinto Shrines in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT::Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu-ku]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Wakamatsu-ku]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web |last=Geocaching |title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site |url=http://www.geocaching.com/ |access-date=2023-10-19 |website=www.geocaching.com |language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:dd]][[en:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]][[Category:Ebisu]] [[Category:Shtines in kitak]] [[Category:Wakamatsu]] [[Category:[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]][[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]][[Category:dd]][[en:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]]]] }} [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E6%9D%BE%E6%81%B5%E6%AF%94%E9%A0%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]]

This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:dd]][[en:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]][[Category:Ebisu]] [[Category:Shtines in kitak]] [[Category:Wakamatsu]] [[Category:[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{citation needed|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{citation needed|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{citation needed|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]][[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]][[Category:dd]][[en:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]]]] }} [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{citation needed|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E6%9D%BE%E6%81%B5%E6%AF%94%E9%A0%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]]

{{draft}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg }} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It worships [[Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine | image = [[File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg|300px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=14}} | location = 1-2-37 Hamamachi, Wakamatsu-ku, [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]
[[Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine]] | festival = [[December 2]] - [[December 4|4]] | ceremony = Ebisu festival }} '''Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine''' (若松恵比須神社, Wakamatsu Ebisu Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Wakamatsu-ku]], [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine and is currently listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Wakamatsu no Oebessan." The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the [[Wakato Bridge]], making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[JR Kyushu]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Shinto Shrines in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu-ku]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Wakamatsu-ku]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web |last=Geocaching |title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site |url=http://www.geocaching.com/ |access-date=2023-10-19 |website=www.geocaching.com |language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:dd]][[en:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]][[Category:Ebisu]] [[Category:Shtines in kitak]] [[Category:Wakamatsu]] [[Category:[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]][[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]][[Category:dd]][[en:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]]]] }} [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E6%9D%BE%E6%81%B5%E6%AF%94%E9%A0%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]]

{{draft}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine | image = [[File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg|300px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=14}} | location = 1-2-37 Hamamachi, Wakamatsu-ku, [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]
[[Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine]] | festival = [[December 2]] - [[December 4|4]] | ceremony = Ebisu festival }} '''Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine''' (若松恵比須神社, Wakamatsu Ebisu Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Wakamatsu-ku]], [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine and is currently listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Wakamatsu no Oebessan." [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It worships [[Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==Overview== The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the [[Wakato Bridge]], making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web|last=Geocaching|title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site|url=http://www.geocaching.com/|access-date=2023-10-19|website=www.geocaching.com|language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[JR Kyushu]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Shinto Shrines in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} revision.comment: adding comment explaining history and blurb thing at the bottom
revision.text:
{{draft}} {{promising draft}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]][[en:draft:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}{{draft}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine | image = [[File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg|300px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=14}} | location = 1-2-37 Hamamachi, Wakamatsu-ku, [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]
[[Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine]] | festival = [[December 2]] - [[December 4|4]] | ceremony = Ebisu festival }} '''Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine''' (若松恵比須神社, Wakamatsu Ebisu Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Wakamatsu-ku]], [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine and is currently listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Wakamatsu no Oebessan." [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It worships [[Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==Overview== The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the [[Wakato Bridge]], making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web|last=Geocaching|title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site|url=http://www.geocaching.com/|access-date=2023-10-19|website=www.geocaching.com|language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[JR Kyushu]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Shinto Shrines in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It worships [[Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==Overview== The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the [[Wakato Bridge]], making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web|last=Geocaching|title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site|url=http://www.geocaching.com/|access-date=2023-10-19|website=www.geocaching.com|language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[JR Kyushu]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web|last=Geocaching|title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site|url=http://www.geocaching.com/|access-date=2023-10-19|website=www.geocaching.com|language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[JR Kyushu]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Shinto Shrines in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[JR Kyushu]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Shinto Shrines in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} revision.comment: moving the draft templates above the comments
revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]][[en:draft:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]][[en:draft:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine | image = [[File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg|300px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=14}} | location = 1-2-37 Hamamachi, Wakamatsu-ku, [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]
[[Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine]] | festival = [[December 2]] - [[December 4|4]] | ceremony = Ebisu festival }} '''Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine''' (若松恵比須神社, Wakamatsu Ebisu Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Wakamatsu-ku]], [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine and is currently listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Wakamatsu no Oebessan." [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It worships [[Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==Overview== The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the [[Wakato Bridge]], making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web|last=Geocaching|title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site|url=http://www.geocaching.com/|access-date=2023-10-19|website=www.geocaching.com|language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[JR Kyushu]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Shinto Shrines in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[JR Kyushu]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links
