[[Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine]] | festival = [[December 2]] - [[December 4|4]] | ceremony = Ebisu festival }} '''Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine''' (若松恵比須神社, Wakamatsu Ebisu Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Wakamatsu-ku]], [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine and is currently listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Wakamatsu no Oebessan." [[{{PAGENAME}]]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It worships [[Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==Overview== The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the [[Wakato Bridge]], making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web|last=Geocaching|title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site|url=http://www.geocaching.com/|access-date=2023-10-19|website=www.geocaching.com|language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[JR Kyushu]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine | image = [[File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg|300px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=14}} | location = 1-2-37 Hamamachi, Wakamatsu-ku, [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]
[[Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine]] | festival = [[December 2]] - [[December 4|4]] | ceremony = Ebisu festival }} '''Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine''' (若松恵比須神社, Wakamatsu Ebisu Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Wakamatsu-ku]], [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine and is currently listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Wakamatsu no Oebessan." [[{{PAGENAME}]]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It worships [[Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==Overview== The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the [[Wakato Bridge]], making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web|last=Geocaching|title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site|url=http://www.geocaching.com/|access-date=2023-10-19|website=www.geocaching.com|language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[JR Kyushu]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[JR Kyushu]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links [[Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine]] | festival = [[December 2]] - [[December 4|4]] | ceremony = Ebisu festival }} '''Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine''' (若松恵比須神社, Wakamatsu Ebisu Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Wakamatsu-ku]], [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine and is currently listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Wakamatsu no Oebessan." [[{{PAGENAME}]]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It worships [[Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==Overview== The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the [[Wakato Bridge]], making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web|last=Geocaching|title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site|url=http://www.geocaching.com/|access-date=2023-10-19|website=www.geocaching.com|language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[JR Kyushu]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine | image = [[File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg|300px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=14}} | location = 1-2-37 Hamamachi, Wakamatsu-ku, [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]
[[Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine]] | festival = [[December 2]] - [[December 4|4]] | ceremony = Ebisu festival }} '''Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine''' (若松恵比須神社, Wakamatsu Ebisu Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Wakamatsu-ku]], [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine and is currently listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Wakamatsu no Oebessan." [[{{PAGENAME}]]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It worships [[Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==Overview== The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the [[Wakato Bridge]], making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web|last=Geocaching|title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site|url=http://www.geocaching.com/|access-date=2023-10-19|website=www.geocaching.com|language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[JR Kyushu]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine | image = [[File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg|300px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=14}} | location = 1-2-37 Hamamachi, Wakamatsu-ku, [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]
[[Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine]] | festival = [[December 2]] - [[December 4|4]] | ceremony = Ebisu festival }} '''Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine''' (若松恵比須神社, Wakamatsu Ebisu Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Wakamatsu-ku]], [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine and is currently listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Wakamatsu no Oebessan." [[{{PAGENAME}]]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It worships [[Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==Overview== The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the [[Wakato Bridge]], making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web|last=Geocaching|title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site|url=http://www.geocaching.com/|access-date=2023-10-19|website=www.geocaching.com|language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[JR Kyushu]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]s in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu-ku]] [[Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine]] | festival = [[December 2]] - [[December 4|4]] | ceremony = Ebisu festival }} '''Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine''' (若松恵比須神社, Wakamatsu Ebisu Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Wakamatsu-ku]], [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine and is currently listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Wakamatsu no Oebessan." [[{{PAGENAME}]]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It worships [[Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==Overview== The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the [[Wakato Bridge]], making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web|last=Geocaching|title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site|url=http://www.geocaching.com/|access-date=2023-10-19|website=www.geocaching.com|language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[JR Kyushu]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| [[Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine]] | festival = [[December 2]] - [[December 4|4]] | ceremony = Ebisu festival }} '''Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine''' (若松恵比須神社, Wakamatsu Ebisu Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Wakamatsu-ku]], [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine and is currently listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Wakamatsu no Oebessan." [[{{PAGENAME}]]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It worships [[Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==Overview== The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the [[Wakato Bridge]], making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web|last=Geocaching|title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site|url=http://www.geocaching.com/|access-date=2023-10-19|website=www.geocaching.com|language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[JR Kyushu]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]s in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu-ku]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine | image = [[File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg|300px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=14}} | location = 1-2-37 Hamamachi, Wakamatsu-ku, [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]
[[Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine]] | festival = [[December 2]] - [[December 4|4]] | ceremony = Ebisu festival }} '''Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine''' (若松恵比須神社, Wakamatsu Ebisu Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Wakamatsu-ku]], [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine and is currently listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Wakamatsu no Oebessan." [[{{PAGENAME}]]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It worships [[Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==Overview== The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the [[Wakato Bridge]], making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web|last=Geocaching|title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site|url=http://www.geocaching.com/|access-date=2023-10-19|website=www.geocaching.com|language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[JR Kyushu]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine | image = [[File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg|300px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=14}} | location = 1-2-37 Hamamachi, Wakamatsu-ku, [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]
[[Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine]] | festival = [[December 2]] - [[December 4|4]] | ceremony = Ebisu festival }} '''Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine''' (若松恵比須神社, Wakamatsu Ebisu Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Wakamatsu-ku]], [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine and is currently listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Wakamatsu no Oebessan." [[{{PAGENAME}]]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It worships [[Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==Overview== The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the [[Wakato Bridge]], making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web|last=Geocaching|title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site|url=http://www.geocaching.com/|access-date=2023-10-19|website=www.geocaching.com|language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[JR Kyushu]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]][[en:draft:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine | image = [[File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg|300px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=14}} | location = 1-2-37 Hamamachi, Wakamatsu-ku, [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]
[[Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine]] | festival = [[December 2]] - [[December 4|4]] | ceremony = Ebisu festival }} '''Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine''' (若松恵比須神社, Wakamatsu Ebisu Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Wakamatsu-ku]], [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine and is currently listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Wakamatsu no Oebessan." [[{{PAGENAME}]]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It worships [[Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==Overview== The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the [[Wakato Bridge]], making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web|last=Geocaching|title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site|url=http://www.geocaching.com/|access-date=2023-10-19|website=www.geocaching.com|language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[JR Kyushu]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]s in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu-ku]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine | image = [[File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg|300px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=14}} | location = 1-2-37 Hamamachi, Wakamatsu-ku, [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]
[[Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine]] | festival = [[December 2]] - [[December 4|4]] | ceremony = Ebisu festival }} '''Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine''' (若松恵比須神社, Wakamatsu Ebisu Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Wakamatsu-ku]], [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine and is currently listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Wakamatsu no Oebessan." [[{{PAGENAME}]]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It worships [[Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==Overview== The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the [[Wakato Bridge]], making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web|last=Geocaching|title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site|url=http://www.geocaching.com/|access-date=2023-10-19|website=www.geocaching.com|language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[JR Kyushu]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[JR Kyushu]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]s in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu-ku]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Wakamatsu-ku]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:dd]][[en:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]][[Category:Ebisu]] [[Category:Shtines in kitak]] [[Category:Wakamatsu]] [[Category: [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}}[[Category:Unsorted]][[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category:tier 2]][[Category:tier 5]][[Category:qq 1]][[Category: Completion categories]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]]

[[Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine]] | festival = [[December 2]] - [[December 4|4]] | ceremony = Ebisu festival }} '''Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine''' (若松恵比須神社, Wakamatsu Ebisu Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Wakamatsu-ku]], [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine and is currently listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Wakamatsu no Oebessan." [[{{PAGENAME}]]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It worships [[Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==Overview== The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the [[Wakato Bridge]], making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web|last=Geocaching|title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site|url=http://www.geocaching.com/|access-date=2023-10-19|website=www.geocaching.com|language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[JR Kyushu]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine | image = [[File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg|300px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=14}} | location = 1-2-37 Hamamachi, Wakamatsu-ku, [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]
[[Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine]] | festival = [[December 2]] - [[December 4|4]] | ceremony = Ebisu festival }} '''Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine''' (若松恵比須神社, Wakamatsu Ebisu Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Wakamatsu-ku]], [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine and is currently listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Wakamatsu no Oebessan." [[{{PAGENAME}]]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It worships [[Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==Overview== The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the [[Wakato Bridge]], making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web|last=Geocaching|title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site|url=http://www.geocaching.com/|access-date=2023-10-19|website=www.geocaching.com|language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[JR Kyushu]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[JR Kyushu]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]][[en:draft:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]][[en:draft:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine | image = [[File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg|300px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=14}} | location = 1-2-37 Hamamachi, Wakamatsu-ku, [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]
[[Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine]] | festival = [[December 2]] - [[December 4|4]] | ceremony = Ebisu festival }} '''Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine''' (若松恵比須神社, Wakamatsu Ebisu Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Wakamatsu-ku]], [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine and is currently listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Wakamatsu no Oebessan." [[{{PAGENAME}]]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It worships [[Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==Overview== The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the [[Wakato Bridge]], making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web|last=Geocaching|title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site|url=http://www.geocaching.com/|access-date=2023-10-19|website=www.geocaching.com|language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[JR Kyushu]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine | image = [[File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg|300px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=14}} | location = 1-2-37 Hamamachi, Wakamatsu-ku, [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]
[[Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine]] | festival = [[December 2]] - [[December 4|4]] | ceremony = Ebisu festival }} '''Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine''' (若松恵比須神社, Wakamatsu Ebisu Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Wakamatsu-ku]], [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine and is currently listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Wakamatsu no Oebessan." [[{{PAGENAME}]]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It worships [[Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==Overview== The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the [[Wakato Bridge]], making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web|last=Geocaching|title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site|url=http://www.geocaching.com/|access-date=2023-10-19|website=www.geocaching.com|language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[JR Kyushu]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]][[en:draft:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]][[en:draft:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine | image = [[File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg|300px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=14}} | location = 1-2-37 Hamamachi, Wakamatsu-ku, [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]
[[Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine]] | festival = [[December 2]] - [[December 4|4]] | ceremony = Ebisu festival }} '''Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine''' (若松恵比須神社, Wakamatsu Ebisu Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Wakamatsu-ku]], [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine and is currently listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Wakamatsu no Oebessan." [[{{PAGENAME}]]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It worships [[Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==Overview== The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the [[Wakato Bridge]], making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web|last=Geocaching|title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site|url=http://www.geocaching.com/|access-date=2023-10-19|website=www.geocaching.com|language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[JR Kyushu]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]s in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu-ku]] {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]][[en:draft:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]][[en:draft:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine | image = [[File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg|300px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=14}} | location = 1-2-37 Hamamachi, Wakamatsu-ku, [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]
[[Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine]] | festival = [[December 2]] - [[December 4|4]] | ceremony = Ebisu festival }} '''Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine''' (若松恵比須神社, Wakamatsu Ebisu Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Wakamatsu-ku]], [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine and is currently listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Wakamatsu no Oebessan." [[{{PAGENAME}]]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It worships [[Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==Overview== The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the [[Wakato Bridge]], making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web|last=Geocaching|title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site|url=http://www.geocaching.com/|access-date=2023-10-19|website=www.geocaching.com|language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[JR Kyushu]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]s in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu-ku]]
