[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine | image = [[File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg|300px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=14}} | location = 1-2-37 Hamamachi, Wakamatsu-ku, [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]
[[Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine]] | festival = [[December 2]] - [[December 4|4]] | ceremony = Ebisu festival }} '''Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine''' (若松恵比須神社, Wakamatsu Ebisu Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Wakamatsu-ku]], [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine and is currently listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Wakamatsu no Oebessan." [[{{PAGENAME}]]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It worships [[Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==Overview== The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the [[Wakato Bridge]], making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web|last=Geocaching|title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site|url=http://www.geocaching.com/|access-date=2023-10-19|website=www.geocaching.com|language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[JR Kyushu]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| [[Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine]] | festival = [[December 2]] - [[December 4|4]] | ceremony = Ebisu festival }} '''Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine''' (若松恵比須神社, Wakamatsu Ebisu Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Wakamatsu-ku]], [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine and is currently listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Wakamatsu no Oebessan." [[{{PAGENAME}]]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It worships [[Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==Overview== The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the [[Wakato Bridge]], making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web|last=Geocaching|title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site|url=http://www.geocaching.com/|access-date=2023-10-19|website=www.geocaching.com|language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[JR Kyushu]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]s in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| {{citation needed|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[JR Kyushu]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine | image = [[File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg|300px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=14}} | location = 1-2-37 Hamamachi, Wakamatsu-ku, [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]
[[Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine]] | festival = [[December 2]] - [[December 4|4]] | ceremony = Ebisu festival }} '''Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine''' (若松恵比須神社, Wakamatsu Ebisu Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Wakamatsu-ku]], [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine and is currently listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Wakamatsu no Oebessan." [[{{PAGENAME}]]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It worships [[Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==Overview== The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the [[Wakato Bridge]], making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web|last=Geocaching|title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site|url=http://www.geocaching.com/|access-date=2023-10-19|website=www.geocaching.com|language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[JR Kyushu]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]s in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| {{citation needed|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu-ku]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Wakamatsu-ku]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Son-sha]] It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine | image = [[File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg|300px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=14}} | location = 1-2-37 Hamamachi, Wakamatsu-ku, [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]
[[Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine]] | festival = [[December 2]] - [[December 4|4]] | ceremony = Ebisu festival }} '''Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine''' (若松恵比須神社, Wakamatsu Ebisu Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Wakamatsu-ku]], [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine and is currently listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Wakamatsu no Oebessan." [[{{PAGENAME}]]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It worships [[Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==Overview== The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the [[Wakato Bridge]], making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web|last=Geocaching|title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site|url=http://www.geocaching.com/|access-date=2023-10-19|website=www.geocaching.com|language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[JR Kyushu]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[JR Kyushu]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links [[Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine]] | festival = [[December 2]] - [[December 4|4]] | ceremony = Ebisu festival }} '''Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine''' (若松恵比須神社, Wakamatsu Ebisu Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Wakamatsu-ku]], [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine and is currently listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Wakamatsu no Oebessan." [[Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It worships [[Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==Overview== The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the [[Wakato Bridge]], making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web|last=Geocaching|title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site|url=http://www.geocaching.com/|access-date=2023-10-19|website=www.geocaching.com|language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[JR Kyushu]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine | image = [[File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg|300px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=14}} | location = 1-2-37 Hamamachi, Wakamatsu-ku, [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]
[[Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine Rank = Village shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine]] | festival = [[December 2]] - [[December 4|4]] | ceremony = Ebisu festival }} '''Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine''' (若松恵比須神社, Wakamatsu Ebisu Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Wakamatsu-ku]], [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The shrine was formerly Ranked as a village shrine and is currently listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Wakamatsu no Oebessan." [[Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It worships [[Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==Overview== The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the [[Wakato Bridge]], making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web|last=Geocaching|title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site|url=http://www.geocaching.com/|access-date=2023-10-19|website=www.geocaching.com|language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[JR Kyushu]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]s in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| {{citation needed|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]]]] }} {{citation needed|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[JR Kyushu]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| revision.comment: 23 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:若松恵比須神社 up to rev 102019209
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=若松恵比須神社 |画像=[[ファイル:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg|260px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[福岡県]][[北九州市]][[若松区]]浜町1丁目2-37 |位置 = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[事代主命]]
[[大国主命]] |社格=旧[[村社]]・[[別表神社]] |例祭=[[12月2日]] - [[12月4日|4日]] |神事=えびす祭 |地図={{Location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=若松
恵比須神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''若松恵比須神社'''（わかまつえびすじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[北九州市]][[若松区]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[近代社格制度|社格]]は村社で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。通称「若松のおえべっさん」。 [[若戸大橋]]の高架橋の真下にあるため、あまり目立たないが、若松の中心街には近い。 == 祭神 == [[事代主|事代主命]]と[[大国主|大国主命]]を主[[祭神]]とし、[[大山咋神]]・[[天照大神|天照皇大御神]]・[[スサノオ|素盞嗚命]]・[[武内宿禰|武内宿禰命]]を配祀する。 == 歴史 == 社伝（『恵比須神社縁起』）によれば、[[仲哀天皇]]と[[神功皇后]]が[[熊襲]]征伐のため洞海から岡津へ船で移動するとき、[[岡県主熊鰐]]が、「天皇様は外海をお進み下さい、皇后様は波静かな内海をお進み下さい。」と奏上したことを受け、皇后の船が内海の洞海湾を進んだとき、急に船が動かなくなったので武内宿禰が海底を調べさせたところ、光り輝く玉の石があがったという。この石こそ海神が皇后の聖旅を守るみしるしの霊石であるとして、畏み勅命を奉じて、祀った祠が当社の始まりであるとする。その後、再びこの地を訪れた武内宿禰が祠脇の聖地に小松を手植えして「海原の滄溟たる松の青々たるわが心も若し」と歓びの歌を詠み、これが「若松」の地名の起こりであるといわれている。 他に、[[村上天皇]]の時代に蛭子大神宮が創建されたという伝承もある。 == 交通 == *[[北九州市交通局|北九州市営バス]] 若松区役所下車 *[[九州旅客鉄道]][[筑豊本線]] [[若松駅]]から徒歩15分 *[[若戸渡船]] 若松渡場から徒歩5分 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == *[http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ 若松恵比須神社公式Webページ] *[https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ 若松恵比須神社公式Blog] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:わかまつえひすしんしや}} [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:福岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:北九州市の神社]] [[Category:若松区の歴史]] [[Category:若松区の建築物]] [[Category:えびす神社]]

{{afc comment|1=see [[every:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} [[Category:Completion tier 7]] {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine | image = [[File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg|300px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=14}} | location = 1-2-37 Hamamachi, Wakamatsu-ku, [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]
[[Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine Rank = Village shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine]] | festival = [[December 2]] - [[December 4|4]] | ceremony = Ebisu festival }} '''Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine''' (若松恵比須神社, Wakamatsu Ebisu Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Wakamatsu-ku]], [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The shrine was formerly Ranked as a village shrine and is currently listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Wakamatsu no Oebessan." [[Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It worships [[Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==Overview== The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the [[Wakato Bridge]], making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web|last=Geocaching|title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site|url=http://www.geocaching.com/|access-date=2023-10-19|website=www.geocaching.com|language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[Emperor Chūai]] and [[Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[JR Kyushu]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]s in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| {{citation needed|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web|last=Geocaching|title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site|url=http://www.geocaching.com/|access-date=2023-10-19|website=www.geocaching.com|language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[:en:Emperor Murakami|Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Wakamatsu Station|Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[:en:Chikuho Main Line|Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]s in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} }} {{translated page|ja|若松恵比須神社|version=102019209|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| [[every:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] [[every:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] [[sn:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E6%9D%BE%E6%81%B5%E6%AF%94%E9%A0%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:akamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu Ward]] {{Commons category|Wakamatsu Ebisu-jinja}} [[ja:若松恵比須神社]]

Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine | image = [[File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg|300px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=14}} | location = 1-2-37 Hamamachi, Wakamatsu-ku, [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]
[[Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine Rank = Village shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine]] | festival = [[:en:December 2|December 2]] - [[:en:December 4|4]] | ceremony = Ebisu festival }} '''Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine''' (若松恵比須神社, Wakamatsu Ebisu Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Wakamatsu-ku]], [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The shrine was formerly Ranked as a village shrine and is currently listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Wakamatsu no Oebessan." [[Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]]] is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It worships [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==Overview== The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the [[:en:Wakato Bridge|Wakato Bridge]], making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web|last=Geocaching|title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site|url=http://www.geocaching.com/|access-date=2023-10-19|website=www.geocaching.com|language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[:en:Emperor Murakami|Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Wakamatsu Station|Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[:en:Chikuho Main Line|Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]s in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} }} {{translated page|ja|若松恵比須神社|version=102019209|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| [[every:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] [[every:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] [[sn:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E6%9D%BE%E6%81%B5%E6%AF%94%E9%A0%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:akamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu Ward]] {{Commons category|Wakamatsu Ebisu-jinja}} [[ja:若松恵比須神社]] [[Category:paes with no wikidata]]

There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[:en:Emperor Murakami|Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Wakamatsu Station|Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[:en:Chikuho Main Line|Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]s in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} }} {{translated page|ja|若松恵比須神社|version=102019209|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| [[every:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] [[every:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] [[sn:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] [[sn:E8%8B%A5%E6%9D%BE%E6%81%B5%E6%AF%94%E9%A0%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:akamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu Ward]] {{Commons category|Wakamatsu Ebisu-jinja}} [[ja:若松恵比須神社]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]]

It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine | image = [[File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg|300px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=14}} | location = 1-2-37 Hamamachi, Wakamatsu-ku, [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]
[[Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine Rank = Village shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine]] | festival = [[:en:December 2|December 2]] - [[:en:December 4|4]] | ceremony = Ebisu festival }} '''Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine''' (若松恵比須神社, Wakamatsu Ebisu Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Wakamatsu-ku]], [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The shrine was formerly Ranked as a village shrine and is currently listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Wakamatsu no Oebessan." [[Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]]] is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It worships [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==Overview== The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the [[:en:Wakato Bridge|Wakato Bridge]], making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web|last=Geocaching|title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site|url=http://www.geocaching.com/|access-date=2023-10-19|website=www.geocaching.com|language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[:en:Emperor Murakami|Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Wakamatsu Station|Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[:en:Chikuho Main Line|Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]s in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} }} {{translated page|ja|若松恵比須神社|version=102019209|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| [[every:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] [[every:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] [[sn:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] [[sn:E8%8B%A5%E6%9D%BE%E6%81%B5%E6%AF%94%E9%A0%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:akamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu Ward]] {{Commons category|Wakamatsu Ebisu-jinja}} [[ja:若松恵比須神社]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]]

There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[:en:Emperor Murakami|Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Wakamatsu Station|Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[:en:Chikuho Main Line|Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]s in Japan| image = File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg| prefecture = {{ill|Wakamatsu-ku|qid=Q858184}}| coordinates = {{Coord|33.905277777777776|N|130.81277777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{Shinto-stub}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} }} {{translated page|ja|若松恵比須神社|version=102019209|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| [[every:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] [[every:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] [[sn:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] [[sn:E8%8B%A5%E6%9D%BE%E6%81%B5%E6%AF%94%E9%A0%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:akamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu Ward]] {{Commons category|Wakamatsu Ebisu-jinja}} [[ja:若松恵比須神社]]

[[Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine Rank = Village shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine]] | festival = [[:en:December 2|December 2]] - [[:en:December 4|4]] | ceremony = Ebisu festival }} '''Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine''' (若松恵比須神社, Wakamatsu Ebisu Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Wakamatsu-ku]], [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The shrine was formerly Ranked as a village shrine and is currently listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Wakamatsu no Oebessan." [[Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]]] is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It worships [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==Overview== The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the [[:en:Wakato Bridge|Wakato Bridge]], making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web|last=Geocaching|title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site|url=http://www.geocaching.com/|access-date=2023-10-19|website=www.geocaching.com|language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[:en:Emperor Murakami|Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Wakamatsu Station|Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[:en:Chikuho Main Line|Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]s in Japan| image = File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg| location = {{ill|Wakamatsu-ku|qid=Q858184}}| coordinates = {{Coord|33.905277777777776|N|130.81277777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{Shinto-stub}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} }} {{translated page|ja|若松恵比須神社|version=102019209|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| [[every:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] [[every:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] [[sn:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] [[sn:E8%8B%A5%E6%9D%BE%E6%81%B5%E6%AF%94%E9%A0%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:akamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu Ward]] {{Commons category|Wakamatsu Ebisu-jinja}} [[ja:若松恵比須神社]]

There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[:en:Emperor Murakami|Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Wakamatsu Station|Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[:en:Chikuho Main Line|Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]s in Japan| image = File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg| location = {{ill|Wakamatsu-ku|qid=Q858184}}| coordinates = {{Coord|33.905277777777776|N|130.81277777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{Shinto-stub}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} }} {{translated page|ja|若松恵比須神社|version=102019209|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| [[every:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] [[every:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] [[sn:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] [[sn:E8%8B%A5%E6%9D%BE%E6%81%B5%E6%AF%94%E9%A0%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:akamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{Commons category|Wakamatsu Ebisu-jinja}} [[ja:若松恵比須神社]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11616405]] Wikidata name: Wakamatsu Ebisu-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Add wikidata]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine | image = [[File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg|300px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=14}} | location = 1-2-37 Hamamachi, Wakamatsu-ku, [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]
[[Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine Rank = Village shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine]] | festival = [[:en:December 2|December 2]] - [[:en:December 4|4]] | ceremony = Ebisu festival }} '''Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine''' (若松恵比須神社, Wakamatsu Ebisu Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Wakamatsu-ku]], [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The shrine was formerly Ranked as a village shrine and is currently listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Wakamatsu no Oebessan." [[Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]]] is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It worships [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==Overview== The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the [[:en:Wakato Bridge|Wakato Bridge]], making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web|last=Geocaching|title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site|url=http://www.geocaching.com/|access-date=2023-10-19|website=www.geocaching.com|language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[:en:Emperor Murakami|Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Wakamatsu Station|Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[:en:Chikuho Main Line|Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]s in Japan| image = File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg| location = {{ill|Wakamatsu-ku|qid=Q858184}}| coordinates = {{Coord|33.905277777777776|N|130.81277777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{Shinto-stub}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} }} {{translated page|ja|若松恵比須神社|version=102019209|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| [[every:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] [[every:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] [[sn:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] [[sn:E8%8B%A5%E6%9D%BE%E6%81%B5%E6%AF%94%E9%A0%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:akamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{Commons category|Wakamatsu Ebisu-jinja}} [[ja:若松恵比須神社]]

There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[:en:Emperor Murakami|Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Wakamatsu Station|Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[:en:Chikuho Main Line|Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]s in Japan| image = File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg| location = {{ill|Wakamatsu-ku|qid=Q858184}}| coordinates = {{Coord|33.905277777777776|N|130.81277777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{Shinto-stub}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} }} {{translated page|ja|若松恵比須神社|version=102019209|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Everybody linked]] {{everybodywiki link|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} [[Category:Everybody linked]] {{everybodywiki link|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu Ward]] {{Commons category|Wakamatsu Ebisu-jinja}} [[ja:若松恵比須神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11616405}}

[[Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine Rank = Village shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine]] | festival = [[:en:December 2|December 2]] - [[:en:December 4|4]] | ceremony = Ebisu festival }} '''Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine''' (若松恵比須神社, Wakamatsu Ebisu Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Wakamatsu-ku]], [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The shrine was formerly Ranked as a village shrine and is currently listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Wakamatsu no Oebessan." [[Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]]] is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It worships [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==Overview== The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the [[:en:Wakato Bridge|Wakato Bridge]], making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web|last=Geocaching|title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site|url=http://www.geocaching.com/|access-date=2023-10-19|website=www.geocaching.com|language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[:en:Emperor Murakami|Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Wakamatsu Station|Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[:en:Chikuho Main Line|Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]s in Japan| image = File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg| location = {{ill|Wakamatsu-ku|WD=Q858184}}| coordinates = {{Coord|33.905277777777776|N|130.81277777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{Shinto-stub}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} }} {{translated page|ja|若松恵比須神社|version=102019209|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Everybody linked]] {{everybodywiki link|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} [[Category:Everybody linked]] {{everybodywiki link|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu Ward]] {{Commons category|Wakamatsu Ebisu-jinja}} [[ja:若松恵比須神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11616405}}

There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[:en:Emperor Murakami|Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Wakamatsu Station|Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[:en:Chikuho Main Line|Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]s in Japan| image = File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg| location = {{ill|Wakamatsu-ku|WD=Q858184}}| coordinates = {{Coord|33.905277777777776|N|130.81277777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{Shinto-stub}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} }} {{translated page|ja|若松恵比須神社|version=102019209|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Everybody linked]] {{everybodywiki link|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} [[Category:Everybody linked]] {{everybodywiki link|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu Ward]] {{Commons category|Wakamatsu Ebisu-jinja}} [[ja:若松恵比須神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11616405}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]
