[[Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine Rank = Village shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine]] | festival = [[:en:December 2|December 2]] - [[:en:December 4|4]] | ceremony = Ebisu festival }} '''Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine''' (若松恵比須神社, Wakamatsu Ebisu Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Wakamatsu-ku]], [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The shrine was formerly Ranked as a village shrine and is currently listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Wakamatsu no Oebessan." [[Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]]] is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It worships [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==Overview== The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the [[:en:Wakato Bridge|Wakato Bridge]], making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web|last=Geocaching|title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site|url=http://www.geocaching.com/|access-date=2023-10-19|website=www.geocaching.com|language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[:en:Emperor Murakami|Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Wakamatsu Station|Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[:en:Chikuho Main Line|Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]s in Japan| image = File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg| location = {{ill|Wakamatsu-ku|WD=Q858184}}| coordinates = {{Coord|33.905277777777776|N|130.81277777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{Shinto-stub}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} }} {{translated page|ja|若松恵比須神社|version=102019209|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Everybody linked]] {{everybodywiki link|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} [[Category:Everybody linked]] {{everybodywiki link|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu Ward]] {{Commons category|Wakamatsu Ebisu-jinja}} [[ja:若松恵比須神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11616405}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[:en:Emperor Murakami|Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Wakamatsu Station|Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[:en:Chikuho Main Line|Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]s in Japan| image = File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg| location = {{ill|Wakamatsu-ku|WD=Q858184}}| coordinates = {{Coord|33.905277777777776|N|130.81277777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{Shinto-stub}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} }} {{translated page|ja|若松恵比須神社|version=102019209|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Everybody linked]] {{everybodywiki link|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} [[Category:Everybody linked]] {{everybodywiki link|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu Ward]] {{Commons category|Wakamatsu Ebisu-jinja}} [[ja:若松恵比須神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11616405}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Wakamatsu Ebisu-jinja]][[ja:若松恵比須神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11616405]] Wikidata name: Wakamatsu Ebisu-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine | image = [[File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg|300px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=14}} | location = 1-2-37 Hamamachi, Wakamatsu-ku, [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]
[[Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine Rank = Village shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine]] | festival = [[:en:December 2|December 2]] - [[:en:December 4|4]] | ceremony = Ebisu festival }} '''Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine''' (若松恵比須神社, Wakamatsu Ebisu Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Wakamatsu-ku]], [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The shrine was formerly Ranked as a village shrine and is currently listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Wakamatsu no Oebessan." [[Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]]] is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It worships [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==Overview== The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the [[:en:Wakato Bridge|Wakato Bridge]], making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web|last=Geocaching|title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site|url=http://www.geocaching.com/|access-date=2023-10-19|website=www.geocaching.com|language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[:en:Emperor Murakami|Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Wakamatsu Station|Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[:en:Chikuho Main Line|Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]s in Japan| image = File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg| location = {{ill|Wakamatsu-ku|WD=Q858184}}| coordinates = {{Coord|33.905277777777776|N|130.81277777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{Shinto-stub}} == References == {{reflist}} }} [[sn:commons:Category:Wakamatsu Ebisu-jinja]][[ja:若松恵比須神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11616405]] Wikidata name: Wakamatsu Ebisu-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine | image = [[File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg|300px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=14}} | location = 1-2-37 Hamamachi, Wakamatsu-ku, [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]
[[Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine Rank = Village shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine]] | festival = [[:en:December 2|December 2]] - [[:en:December 4|4]] | ceremony = Ebisu festival }} '''Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine''' (若松恵比須神社, Wakamatsu Ebisu Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Wakamatsu-ku]], [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The shrine was formerly Ranked as a village shrine and is currently listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Wakamatsu no Oebessan." [[Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]]] is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It worships [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==Overview== The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the [[:en:Wakato Bridge|Wakato Bridge]], making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web|last=Geocaching|title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site|url=http://www.geocaching.com/|access-date=2023-10-19|website=www.geocaching.com|language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[:en:Emperor Murakami|Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Wakamatsu Station|Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[:en:Chikuho Main Line|Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]s in Japan| image = File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg| location = {{ill|Wakamatsu-ku|WD=Q858184}}| coordinates = {{Coord|33.905277777777776|N|130.81277777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{Shinto-stub}} == References == {{reflist}} }} There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[:en:Emperor Murakami|Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Wakamatsu Station|Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[:en:Chikuho Main Line|Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]s in Japan| image = File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg| location = {{ill|Wakamatsu-ku|WD=Q858184}}| coordinates = {{Coord|33.905277777777776|N|130.81277777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{Shinto-stub}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} }} {{translated page|ja|若松恵比須神社|version=102019209|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Everybody linked]] {{everybodywiki link|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} [[Category:Everybody linked]] {{everybodywiki link|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu Ward]] {{Commons category|Wakamatsu Ebisu-jinja}} [[ja:若松恵比須神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11616405}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11616405}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Wakamatsu Ebisu-jinja]][[ja:若松恵比須神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11616405]] Wikidata name: Wakamatsu Ebisu-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine | image = [[File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg|300px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=14}} | location = 1-2-37 Hamamachi, Wakamatsu-ku, [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]
[[Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine Rank = Village shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine]] | festival = [[:en:December 2|December 2]] - [[:en:December 4|4]] | ceremony = Ebisu festival }} '''Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine''' (若松恵比須神社, Wakamatsu Ebisu Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Wakamatsu-ku]], [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The shrine was formerly Ranked as a village shrine and is currently listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Wakamatsu no Oebessan." [[Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]]] is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It worships [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==Overview== The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the [[:en:Wakato Bridge|Wakato Bridge]], making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web|last=Geocaching|title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site|url=http://www.geocaching.com/|access-date=2023-10-19|website=www.geocaching.com|language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[:en:Emperor Murakami|Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Wakamatsu Station|Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[:en:Chikuho Main Line|Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]s in Japan| image = File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg| location = {{ill|Wakamatsu-ku|WD=Q858184}}| coordinates = {{Coord|33.905277777777776|N|130.81277777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{Shinto-stub}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} }} {{translated page|ja|若松恵比須神社|version=102019209|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Everybody linked]] {{everybodywiki link|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} [[Category:Everybody linked]] {{everybodywiki link|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu Ward]] {{Commons category|Wakamatsu Ebisu-jinja}} [[ja:若松恵比須神社]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11616405}}

There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[:en:Emperor Murakami|Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Wakamatsu Station|Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[:en:Chikuho Main Line|Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]s in Japan| image = File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg| location = {{ill|Wakamatsu-ku|WD=Q858184}}| coordinates = {{Coord|33.905277777777776|N|130.81277777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{Shinto-stub}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} }} {{translated page|ja|若松恵比須神社|version=102019209|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Everybody linked]] {{everybodywiki link|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} [[Category:Everybody linked]] {{everybodywiki link|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu Ward]] {{Commons category|Wakamatsu Ebisu-jinja}} [[ja:若松恵比須神社]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11616405}}

It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine | image = [[File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg|300px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=14}} | location = 1-2-37 Hamamachi, Wakamatsu-ku, [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]
[[Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine Rank = Village shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine]] | festival = [[:en:December 2|December 2]] - [[:en:December 4|4]] | ceremony = Ebisu festival }} '''Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine''' (若松恵比須神社, Wakamatsu Ebisu Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Wakamatsu-ku]], [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The shrine was formerly Ranked as a village shrine and is currently listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Wakamatsu no Oebessan." [[Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]]] is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It worships [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==Overview== The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the [[:en:Wakato Bridge|Wakato Bridge]], making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web|last=Geocaching|title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site|url=http://www.geocaching.com/|access-date=2023-10-19|website=www.geocaching.com|language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[:en:Emperor Murakami|Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Wakamatsu Station|Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[:en:Chikuho Main Line|Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]s in Japan| image = File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg| location = {{ill|Wakamatsu-ku|WD=Q858184}}| coordinates = {{Coord|33.905277777777776|N|130.81277777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{Shinto-stub}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} }} {{translated page|ja|若松恵比須神社|version=102019209|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Everybody linked]] {{everybodywiki link|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} [[Category:Everybody linked]] {{everybodywiki link|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu Ward]] {{Commons category|Wakamatsu Ebisu-jinja}} [[ja:若松恵比須神社]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11616405}} [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[:en:Emperor Murakami|Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Wakamatsu Station|Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[:en:Chikuho Main Line|Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]s in Japan| image = File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg| location = {{ill|Wakamatsu-ku|WD=Q858184}}| coordinates = {{Coord|33.905277777777776|N|130.81277777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{Shinto-stub}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} }} {{translated page|ja|若松恵比須神社|version=102019209|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{everybodywiki link|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} {{everybodywiki link|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} {{Commons category|Wakamatsu Ebisu-jinja}} [[ja:若松恵比須神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11616405}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:Everybody linked]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. [[Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine Rank = Village shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine]] | festival = [[:en:December 2|December 2]] - [[:en:December 4|4]] | ceremony = Ebisu festival }} '''Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine''' (若松恵比須神社, Wakamatsu Ebisu Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Wakamatsu-ku]], [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The shrine was formerly Ranked as a village shrine and is currently listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Wakamatsu no Oebessan." [[Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]]] is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It worships [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==Overview== The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the [[:en:Wakato Bridge|Wakato Bridge]], making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web|last=Geocaching|title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site|url=http://www.geocaching.com/|access-date=2023-10-19|website=www.geocaching.com|language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[:en:Emperor Murakami|Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Wakamatsu Station|Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[:en:Chikuho Main Line|Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]s in Japan| image = File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg| location = {{ill|Wakamatsu-ku|WD=Q858184}}| coordinates = {{Coord|33.905277777777776|N|130.81277777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{Shinto-stub}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} }} {{translated page|ja|若松恵比須神社|version=102019209|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{everybodywiki link|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} {{everybodywiki link|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} {{Commons category|Wakamatsu Ebisu-jinja}} [[ja:若松恵比須神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11616405}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:Everybody linked]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[:en:Emperor Murakami|Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Wakamatsu Station|Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[:en:Chikuho Main Line|Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]s in Japan| image = File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg| location = {{ill|Wakamatsu-ku|WD=Q858184}}| coordinates = {{Coord|33.905277777777776|N|130.81277777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{Shinto-stub}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} }} {{translated page|ja|若松恵比須神社|version=102019209|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{everybodywiki link|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} {{everybodywiki link|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} {{Commons category|Wakamatsu Ebisu-jinja}} [[ja:若松恵比須神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11616405}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:Everybody linked]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Wakamatsu Ebisu-jinja]][[ja:若松恵比須神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Wakamatsu Ebisu-jinja]][[ja:若松恵比須神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Wakamatsu Ebisu-jinja]][[ja:若松恵比須神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11616405]] Wikidata name: Wakamatsu Ebisu-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine | image = [[File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg|300px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=14}} | location = 1-2-37 Hamamachi, Wakamatsu-ku, [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]
[[Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine Rank = Village shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine]] | festival = [[:en:December 2|December 2]] - [[:en:December 4|4]] | ceremony = Ebisu festival }} '''Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine''' (若松恵比須神社, Wakamatsu Ebisu Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Wakamatsu-ku]], [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The shrine was formerly Ranked as a village shrine and is currently listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Wakamatsu no Oebessan." [[Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]]] is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It worships [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==Overview== The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the [[:en:Wakato Bridge|Wakato Bridge]], making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web|last=Geocaching|title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site|url=http://www.geocaching.com/|access-date=2023-10-19|website=www.geocaching.com|language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[:en:Emperor Murakami|Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Wakamatsu Station|Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[:en:Chikuho Main Line|Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]s in Japan| image = File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg| location = {{ill|Wakamatsu-ku|WD=Q858184}}| coordinates = {{Coord|33.905277777777776|N|130.81277777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{Shinto-stub}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} }} {{translated page|ja|若松恵比須神社|version=102019209|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{everybodywiki link|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} {{everybodywiki link|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} {{Commons category|Wakamatsu Ebisu-jinja}} [[ja:若松恵比須神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11616405}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:Everybody linked]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]

There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of [[:en:Emperor Murakami|Emperor Murakami]]. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Wakamatsu Station|Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[:en:Chikuho Main Line|Chikuho Main Line]], operated by [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]s in Japan| image = File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg| location = {{ill|Wakamatsu-ku|WD=Q858184}}| coordinates = {{Coord|33.905277777777776|N|130.81277777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{Shinto-stub}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} }} {{translated page|ja|若松恵比須神社|version=102019209|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{everybodywiki link|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} {{everybodywiki link|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} {{Commons category|Wakamatsu Ebisu-jinja}} [[ja:若松恵比須神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11616405}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:Everybody linked]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Wakamatsu Ebisu-jinja]][[ja:若松恵比須神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Wakamatsu Ebisu-jinja]][[ja:若松恵比須神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Wakamatsu Ebisu-jinja]][[ja:若松恵比須神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11616405]] Wikidata name: Wakamatsu Ebisu-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine | image = [[File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg|300px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=14}} | location = 1-2-37 Hamamachi, Wakamatsu-ku, {{ill|Kitakyushu|en|Kitakyushu|WD=Q188806|lt=Kitakyushu}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}} | coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]
[[Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine Rank = Village shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine]] | festival = {{ill|December 2|en|December 2|WD=Q2296|lt=December 2}} - {{ill|December 4|en|December 4|WD=Q2310|lt=4}} | ceremony = Ebisu festival }} '''Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine''' (若松恵比須神社, Wakamatsu Ebisu Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Wakamatsu-ku]], {{ill|Kitakyushu|en|Kitakyushu|WD=Q188806|lt=Kitakyushu}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. The shrine was formerly Ranked as a village shrine and is currently listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Wakamatsu no Oebessan." [[Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]]] is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It worships {{ill|Ebisu (mythology)|en|Ebisu (mythology)|WD=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu}} ==Overview== The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the {{ill|Wakato Bridge|en|Wakato Bridge|WD=Q7960851|lt=Wakato Bridge}}, making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}} visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web|last=Geocaching|title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site|url=http://www.geocaching.com/|access-date=2023-10-19|website=www.geocaching.com|language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}} were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of {{ill|Emperor Murakami|en|Emperor Murakami|WD=Q374015|lt=Emperor Murakami}}. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Wakamatsu Station|en|Wakamatsu Station|WD=Q7960787|lt=Wakamatsu Station}} on the [[:en:Chikuho Main Line|Chikuho Main Line]], operated by {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]s in Japan| image = File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg| location = {{ill|Wakamatsu-ku|WD=Q858184}}| coordinates = {{Coord|33.905277777777776|N|130.81277777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{Shinto-stub}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} }} {{translated page|ja|若松恵比須神社|version=102019209|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{everybodywiki link|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} {{everybodywiki link|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} {{Commons category|Wakamatsu Ebisu-jinja}} [[ja:若松恵比須神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11616405}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:Everybody linked]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]

This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web|last=Geocaching|title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site|url=http://www.geocaching.com/|access-date=2023-10-19|website=www.geocaching.com|language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}} were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of {{ill|Emperor Murakami|en|Emperor Murakami|WD=Q374015|lt=Emperor Murakami}}. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Wakamatsu Station|en|Wakamatsu Station|WD=Q7960787|lt=Wakamatsu Station}} on the [[:en:Chikuho Main Line|Chikuho Main Line]], operated by {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]s in Japan| image = File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg| location = {{ill|Wakamatsu-ku|WD=Q858184}}| coordinates = {{Coord|33.905277777777776|N|130.81277777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{Shinto-stub}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} }} {{translated page|ja|若松恵比須神社|version=102019209|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{everybodywiki link|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} {{everybodywiki link|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} {{Commons category|Wakamatsu Ebisu-jinja}} [[ja:若松恵比須神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11616405}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:Everybody linked]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]

Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Wakamatsu Station|en|Wakamatsu Station|WD=Q7960787|lt=Wakamatsu Station|1=Wakamatsu Station}} on the [[:en:Chikuho Main Line|Chikuho Main Line]], operated by {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]s in Japan| image = File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg| location = {{ill|Wakamatsu-ku|WD=Q858184|1=Wakamatsu-ku, Kitakyūshū}}| coordinates = {{Coord|33.905277777777776|N|130.81277777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{Shinto-stub}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} }} {{translated page|ja|若松恵比須神社|version=102019209|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{everybodywiki link|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} {{everybodywiki link|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} {{Commons category|Wakamatsu Ebisu-jinja}} [[ja:若松恵比須神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11616405}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:Everybody linked]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Wakamatsu Ebisu-jinja]][[ja:若松恵比須神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Wakamatsu Ebisu-jinja]][[ja:若松恵比須神社]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Wakamatsu Ebisu-jinja]][[ja:若松恵比須神社]] {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine | image = [[File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg|300px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=14}} | location = 1-2-37 Hamamachi, Wakamatsu-ku, {{ill|Kitakyushu|en|Kitakyushu|WD=Q188806|lt=Kitakyushu|1=Kitakyushu}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}} | coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]
[[Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine Rank = Village shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine]] | festival = {{ill|December 2|en|December 2|WD=Q2296|lt=December 2|1=December 2}} - {{ill|December 4|en|December 4|WD=Q2310|lt=4|1=December 4}} | ceremony = Ebisu festival }} '''Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine''' (若松恵比須神社, Wakamatsu Ebisu Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Wakamatsu-ku]], {{ill|Kitakyushu|en|Kitakyushu|WD=Q188806|lt=Kitakyushu|1=Kitakyushu}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. The shrine was formerly Ranked as a village shrine and is currently listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Wakamatsu no Oebessan." [[Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine]]] is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It worships {{ill|Ebisu (mythology)|en|Ebisu (mythology)|WD=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu|1=Ebisu (mythology)}} ==Overview== The shrine is situated directly under the elevated structure of the {{ill|Wakato Bridge|en|Wakato Bridge|WD=Q7960851|lt=Wakato Bridge|1=Wakato Bridge}}, making it somewhat inconspicuous, although it is close to the central area of Wakamatsu. == deity == The primary deities enshrined are [[Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Ōkuninushi]], with [[Ōyamakui no Kami]], [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Mikoto]], and [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] also enshrined. == History == The shrine is about 1,800 years old. {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} visited Tsukusinokuni. During the visit, warlord Takeuchinosukune found a shining stone in the sea. They brought it to the nearby beach to worship it. This stone is still in the main shrine.{{Cite web|last=Geocaching|title=Geocaching - The Official Global GPS Cache Hunt Site|url=http://www.geocaching.com/|access-date=2023-10-19|website=www.geocaching.com|language=en-US}} Takeuchinosukune also planted a pine tree at the shrine. He sang a song about seeing the blue sea and the green pine tree. This song is believed to be why the place is called "Wakamatsu." According to shrine legend (as recorded in 'Ebisu Shrine Engi'), when {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} were traveling by boat from Dōkai to Okatsu to subjugate the [[Kumaso]], Okatsunushi Kumawani advised, "Your Majesty, please proceed through the open sea, while Her Majesty should proceed through the calm inland sea." When the Empress's boat advanced through the Dōkai Bay, it suddenly stopped. Takenouchi no Sukune investigated the sea floor and discovered a shining stone. This stone was believed to be a sacred stone sent by the sea god to protect the Empress's holy journey. Thus, the stone was enshrined, marking the beginning of the shrine. Later, when Takenouchi no Sukune visited the place again, he planted a small pine tree next to the sacred site and composed a poem expressing his joy, which led to the naming of the area as "Wakamatsu" (meaning "young pine"). There is also a tradition that a shrine dedicated to Hiruko Daijingu was established during the era of {{ill|Emperor Murakami|en|Emperor Murakami|WD=Q374015|lt=Emperor Murakami|1=Emperor Murakami}}. == Transportation == * [[Kitakyushu City Bus]] - Get off at Wakamatsu Ward Office * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Wakamatsu Station|en|Wakamatsu Station|WD=Q7960787|lt=Wakamatsu Station|1=Wakamatsu Station}} on the [[:en:Chikuho Main Line|Chikuho Main Line]], operated by {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} * 5-minute walk from Wakamatsu Ferry Terminal, served by [[Wakato Ferry]] == External Links == * [http://wakamatsu-ebisu.jp/ Official Website of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] * [https://ebisujinja.exblog.jp/ Official Blog of Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]s in Japan| image = File:Wakamatsu-Ebisu-jinja.jpg| location = {{ill|Wakamatsu-ku|WD=Q858184|1=Wakamatsu-ku, Kitakyūshū}}| coordinates = {{Coord|33.905277777777776|N|130.81277777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{Shinto-stub}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} }} {{translated page|ja|若松恵比須神社|version=102019209|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{everybodywiki link|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} {{everybodywiki link|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine|Wakamatsu Ebisu Shrine}} {{Commons category|Wakamatsu Ebisu-jinja}} [[ja:若松恵比須神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11616405}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:Everybody linked]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:pages]]
